entries.notes: Runner evidence authority lands with baseline and mutant runner work.
Candidate determinism requires AST candidate ordering and mutant model tests.

Reports sort mutants by canonical order (file, byte start/end, operator, replacement, backend) before assigning display ids and summary counts; report_renderers_test exercises out-of-order mutants through sortAndAssignDisplayIds and asserts the sorted order and 1..N display ids, and the renderer snapshots emit canonical order.
AI boundary tests land with stub provider and context schema work.
Backend default enforcement is first covered by config and version-policy tests.
Pinned Zig 0.16.0 checks land with Zig version policy.
No permanent worktree mutation is covered by sandbox tests.
Original-text span validation is covered by patch sandbox tests.
Test declaration exclusion is covered by AST traversal fixtures.
Compile-error classification lands with mutant runner tests.
Invalid mutant semantics start with the shared mutant model and continue through sandbox tests.

same_file_then_package selection narrows execution to a generated `zig test <file>` command but the mutant still appears in the report with its result status: the run-integration test asserts the killed mutant is present (mutants.len==1) and report.validate is ok, and selection records strategy/selected/commands/preflight_commands/fallback_used so no executed mutant is hidden.

Cache keys include every deterministic input from docs/PERFORMANCE_STRATEGY.md: zentinel version, Zig version, Zig cache namespace metadata, backend name and backend_version (independently), operator, source content hash, config hash, test command, mode, and environment normalization. cache_key_test asserts the key is stable across repeated computation and that changing any one of these inputs changes the key, and backend_version changes the key independently of the display backend name.
Task and schema registry versions are validated by the bootstrap validator; report and AI schema contract tests land later.

Snapshot outputs normalize durations, ids, and timestamps: report_renderers_test asserts the JUnit renderer emits time="0" (no raw duration leaks) and that report.normalizeForComparison replaces run id, started_at, and every duration_ms with <run-id>/<started-at>/<duration> for repeated-run comparison. Doctest snapshot normalization remains tracked by its own invariants under the doctest tasks.

Doctest deterministic case IDs: the extractor derives a durable `dt_` id (sha256 + lowercase Crockford base32, 26 chars) from the project-relative doc path, case kind, explicit label, normalized block grouping metadata, and grouped-block content hash, deliberately excluding line numbers. Tests pin a case-id snapshot, prove the id changes with grouped content and labels but is stable under unrelated surrounding prose, and that repeated extraction is byte-deterministic.
The validator enforces at most one active pending-completion task and rejects implemented/verified task-control states.
The validator checks Markdown and JSON task queue/status consistency.
Pipeline artifact role timestamps will prove failing evidence was recorded before implementation.
The validator rejects prose-only follow-up bullets unless they explicitly state no predefined follow-up exists, and validates concrete task references in task Markdown.
Compiler crash classification lands with mutant runner tests.
The validator requires ADR-0008 architecture-boundary contracts and checks source layer declarations plus deterministic-core import boundaries once src/**/*.zig files exist.
